summary: High-risk regulatory changes for food seasoning/snack compliance include: Codex updated contaminant limits, establishing new Maximum Levels for toxicants, mycotoxins, metals, and radionuclides. The GCC/GSO introduced new mandatory labelling for pre-packaged foodstuffs, Halal claims, and shelf life, plus new microbiological criteria. Japan implemented a stricter positive list for additives, new production/processing standards, prohibitions on harmful contaminants/toxic substances, limits for agricultural chemical residues, and new certification rules for imported livestock products and pathogens. Singapore now mandates compliance with SFA-assessed additive and contaminant limits, prohibiting non-permitted substances or those exceeding limits, and requires full ingredient/additive declaration. Taiwan updated its mandatory allergen declaration list for 10 key allergens. Thailand introduced new contaminant residue limits (ASEAN MRLs) and requires verification of prohibited foods. In the United States, major food allergens (including sesame) must be declared, alongside new labelling rules for 'gluten-free' claims, allergen preventive controls, cochineal extract, carmine, FD&C Yellow No. 5, and sulfiting agents, with continued compliance for poisonous substances.

changes.oldLimit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and CCCF established levels
changes.newLimit: Maximum levels (MLs) or guideline levels must be adhered to. Verification required via relevant Codex texts/standards.
changes.notes: Contaminants are substances not intentionally added to food. Codex MLs are established to protect human health and are kept as low as possible based on scientific evidence. The General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) is a key reference.

changes.newLimit: Maximum levels (MLs) must be adhered to. Verification required via relevant Codex texts/standards.
changes.notes: Codex has established MLs for specific contaminants like mycotoxins, metals (arsenic, lead, mercury), and radionuclides. These MLs ensure food does not contain contaminants at levels threatening human health.

changes.parameter: General labelling requirements for pre-packaged foodstuffs
changes.notes: This technical regulation covers general labelling requirements for pre-packaged food products, including information such as ingredients, nutritional facts, and origin.

changes.parameter: Halal food general requirements (for products making a Halal claim)
changes.notes: This technical regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, which impacts ingredient selection and processing, and must be adhered to if a Halal claim is made on the label.
This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products that must be declared on the label.
Specific labelling requirements for prepackaged food stuffs must be verified within the full text of GSO 9:2022.
Specific general requirements for Halal food must be verified within the full text of GSO 2055-1:2015.
Specific mandatory expiration date requirements for food products must be verified within the full text of GSO 150-1:2013.
Specific microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients must be verified within the full text of GSO 1016:2015.

changes.parameter: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives)
changes.newLimit: Prohibited unless MHLW specifies as unlikely to cause harm
changes.notes: This implies a positive list system for additives.

changes.newLimit: Must conform to MHLW established criteria for production, processing, use, cooking, preservation methods, and standards for ingredients
changes.notes: Verification against specific MHLW standards and criteria is required.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm to human health

changes.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceuticals for animals
changes.notes: This refers to the Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues.

changes.newLimit: Prohibited, unless MHLW specifies as unlikely to cause harm

changes.parameter: Imported livestock meat, etc. with diseases/abnormalities
changes.newLimit: Must have certificates from exporting country's governmental organization certifying freedom from specified diseases/abnormalities, or MHLW-specified electronic data
Prohibited if contaminated or suspected to be so and likely to cause harm

changes.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for food additives
changes.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply.

changes.newLimit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA
changes.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of an additive not currently permitted.

changes.parameter: Compliance with regulatory limits for contaminants
changes.notes: All food businesses must comply with these limits and also with Regulatory Limits for Food Additives.

changes.oldLimit: Not allowed to exceed regulatory limits
changes.newLimit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits

changes.notes: Prohibited under Regulation 33 of the Food Regulations.
Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

changes.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
changes.newLimit: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
changes.notes: These are 10 of the 11 mandatory allergens requiring declaration.
The updated ASEAN MRLs list must be consulted for maximum residue limits.
A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale must be verified.
Listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS'.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on the label
changes.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
This is an action level.

changes.oldLimit: Verification required via Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance/regulations
changes.newLimit: Must comply with tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels
changes.notes: Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. Users must refer to the transparency tool and linked guidance documents for specific contaminant-commodity pairs.

changes.newLimit: Must meet FDA's defined standard for gluten content
changes.notes: Specific compliance requirements exist for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (final rule August 12, 2020).

changes.newLimit: Establishments must put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per 21 CFR part 117 (CGMP & PC rule)
changes.notes: This rule establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared on the label
changes.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.

changes.newLimit: Must be declared in one of two ways: 1) In parentheses following the ingredient name, or 2) Immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement
changes.notes: The type of tree nut, species of fish, and species of Crustacean shellfish must also be declared.
Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is at or above the limit. Required due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.

changes.newLimit: Verification required via GSFA online database
changes.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpreting the data. The database is regularly updated.
